title: diplolaena velutina
intro: Diplolaena velutina is a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae, commonly known as the velvet bushwillow or hairy bushwillow. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever and rheumatic conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Diplolaena velutina include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant are often made by infusing the leaves in hot water or using the root in decoctions to harness its medicinal properties.
